ping reviewers

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

> It looks like https://review.whamcloud.com/31875 may be addressing the same problem.

Yes, the root reason is the zero record that is created when initializing the IAM object. Both the two patches can handle new created IAM, but we still need to handle the upgraded case.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

* Code-Review-1 by HPDD Checkpatch (377)

parents.subject: LU-10858 build: handle yaml library packaging on SLES systems

commit.subject: LU-10865 osd-ldiskfs: bump quota index version initially
commit.message:
LU-10865 osd-ldiskfs: bump quota index version initially

One of customer reported following dmesg error:

Mar 29 01:30:20 xssds31 kernel: Lustre: 2007:0:
(qsd_reint.c:246:qsd_reint_index())
lustre3-MDT0000: index version for fid [0x200000005:0x1016:0x0]
is 0, but index isn't empty (1)

This is easy to reproduce:
1.format Lustre and mount MDT/MGS, OST.
2.lctl conf_param lustre.quota.mdt=ug
3.lctl set_param osd-ldiskfs.*.quota_slave.force_reint=1

Then you will see some dmesg like above.
Problem is osd-ldiskfs IAM will try to init one
zero entry initially, so for newly created slave index
file, there will be at least one entry for it, let's
bump version properly to make reintegration check
happy

Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-ldiskfs testlist=sanity-quota \
	mdtfilesystemtype=ldiskfs ostfilesystemtype=ldiskfs

Change-Id: I5935f930e1f33041020636f68fca5f725ccea057
Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
